text:
byGEOFF HARRIS | The National Desk

TOPICS:

WASHINGTON (TND) —Several U.S. military bases across Europe are now operating under heightened alert.

The alert is calledForce Protection Condition "Charlie."It is the second highest Force Protection Condition behind Delta.

A retired military intelligence officer with 20 years of active service, Michael Wallace experienced a "Charlie" alert while serving overseas in Africa.

According to Wallace, when one of these alerts comes in, it's a big deal because it's like notifying a mini city.

"If you're checking every car for an IED and you have thousands of people coming on base, that's going to take a while," Wallace said.

A professor of emergency and security studies at Tulane University,Wallace said that this "Charlie" alert may have been issued because U.S. forces found a credible threat.

"There are a lot of possible bad guys out there right now that are looking to possibly strike the United States," said Wallace.

A PhD candidate in the School of Public Affairs at American University Sara Harmouch said another factor behind the alert could be sporting events happening in Europe.

Until the possible terror threat or target is resolved, Harmouch said the alert will likely stay in effect.

"This alert will probably last, I'm assuming, until August, up until further notice," said Harmouch.

The development was first reported by "Stars and Stripes,"an independent military publication.
title: US military bases in Europe issue new alert: FPCON 'Charlie'
author: GEOFF HARRIS | The National Desk
description: Several U.S. military bases across Europe are now operating under heightened alert.
keywords: FPCON Charlie,U.S. military bases,Europe,Credible threat,Heightened alert,Surveillance,Security measures,Terror threat
